title: Epicranthes cimicina
intro: Epicranthes cimicina, also known as the 'Snake's Head Lily', is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Epicranthes cimicina include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Epicranthes cimicina include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its therapeutic benefits.
taxonomy: Epicranthes cimicina is classified within the family Acanthaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as acanthus or thorny flowers.
